title: BOOM Molecular OOD Benchmark Datasets

data_urls.description: BOOM repository with benchmark data instructions
data_urls.access_notes: Follow upstream instructions; do not assume small downloads.

instructions: Clone or inspect the BOOM repository.
Follow upstream data-download instructions for the desired task.
Verify train/validation/test split labels and molecule identifiers before constructing SPECTRA splits.

recommended_axes.name: chemical_distance
recommended_axes.definition: Morgan fingerprint Tanimoto or another molecule fingerprint similarity to train.

recommended_axes.name: scaffold_novelty
recommended_axes.definition: Bemis-Murcko scaffold identity or scaffold fingerprint similarity.

recommended_axes.name: property_extremeness
recommended_axes.definition: Distance from training target-property support; diagnostic or post-hoc unless used only on training labels for split construction.

useful_for: Testing whether chemical novelty explains property prediction degradation.
Sanity-checking SPECTRA split and curve machinery.
leakage_risks: Target-property support is post-hoc if computed with held-out labels.
SMILES duplicates, salts, stereochemistry, and canonicalization can create hidden leakage.
Random and scaffold split labels should be treated as operating points, not the full curve.
